description: Project scaffolding — monorepo with ts-executor/ and py-engine/ services, shared/schemas/ for message contracts, Docker Compose config, and local dev tooling
steps: ts-executor/ builds and runs with TypeScript strict mode, no errors
py-engine/ runs with no import errors; dependencies locked in requirements.txt or pyproject.toml
shared/schemas/ contains at least one JSON schema that both services can validate against
docker-compose.yml brings up Redis + both services with a single command
README with setup instructions that a fresh machine can follow end-to-end
.env.example documents all required environment variables

description: Redis communication layer — Redis 7+ as sole inter-service channel with three primary channels: market:events (TS→Python), execution:orders (Python→TS), execution:results (TS→Python), plus cache layer for prices, gas, and pool states
steps: Pub/sub messaging works bidirectionally between Python and TS services
Redis Streams configured with TTL-based pruning (configurable expiry window)
Cache layer operational for prices, gas, and pool states with configurable TTLs
Both services validate all messages against shared JSON schemas before processing
Schema violations are rejected with structured error logs, not silently consumed
Connection loss to Redis triggers graceful pause in both services without crash or orphaned state

description: JSON schema contracts — structured message format definitions for every Redis channel, serving as the contract between Python brain and TypeScript hands
steps: Schemas defined for market:events, execution:orders, and execution:results
Each schema includes required fields, types, and valid value ranges
execution:orders schema enforces: token, amount, slippage ceiling, gas ceiling, deadline
Both Python and TS services validate inbound messages at the boundary
Test suite sends malformed messages and confirms rejection with appropriate errors
Schemas include a version field in every message for forward compatibility

description: Docker Compose local development — single-command dev environment mirroring production topology with hot-reload for both services
steps: docker compose up starts all three containers (Redis, py-engine, ts-executor)
Source code changes trigger automatic reload in both services
Logs from all services visible in a single terminal with service-name prefixes
Environment variables injected via .env file, not hardcoded
Container networking allows both services to reach Redis by hostname

description: Alchemy WebSocket subscription manager — persistent WebSocket connections for real-time blockchain event monitoring with auto-reconnection and event parsing
steps: WebSocket connection to Alchemy established on service startup
Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff on disconnection
Subscribes to relevant events: pending transactions, new blocks, Aave contract events
Raw events parsed into normalized internal format before publishing to Redis
Connection health monitored; prolonged disconnection triggers alert
Alchemy rate limits handled gracefully with backpressure, not crash

description: Market event publisher — transforms raw blockchain events into structured market events and publishes to market:events Redis channel
steps: Events published to market:events conform to shared JSON schema
Each event includes: event type, chain, protocol, token addresses, amounts, block number, timestamp
Events are deduplicated (same TX doesn't produce duplicate events)
Publishing latency from event detection to Redis is under 100ms
Events include a monotonically increasing sequence number for ordering

description: viem transaction builder — consumes orders from execution:orders, constructs and submits Ethereum transactions via viem, enforces gas/slippage/deadline parameters set by Python risk manager
steps: Consumes orders from execution:orders Redis channel
Constructs transactions with correct ABI encoding for target protocols
Enforces gas ceiling, slippage bounds, and deadline from order; rejects if on-chain conditions exceed
Signs transactions using configured wallet
Publishes result (hash, status, fill price, actual gas) to execution:results
Nonce management prevents stuck transactions from nonce gaps
Failed transactions retried with configurable backoff, max 3 retries

description: Alchemy Smart Wallet integration — Account Abstraction wallet with on-chain spending limits, contract allowlists, and session keys for programmable guardrails
steps: Smart wallet deployed and configured on Sepolia testnet
On-chain spending limits enforced (daily and per-transaction caps)
Contract allowlist restricts interactions to approved protocol contracts only
Wallet balance and nonce queryable on demand
Gas sponsorship or bundled UserOps work correctly via ERC-4337 flow
Private key stored securely via environment variable, never in code

description: Flashbots Protect integration — route swap and arbitrage transactions through private mempool to prevent frontrunning and sandwich attacks
steps: Swap transactions submitted to Flashbots Protect RPC endpoint, not public mempool
Transaction status polled until inclusion or timeout
Fallback to public mempool if Flashbots unreachable, with alert
Configurable per-transaction: some strategies can opt out of private routing
Latency overhead of Flashbots routing logged and monitored

description: Aave V3 protocol adapter — supply, withdraw, and query market data (utilization rates, supply APY, available liquidity) on Aave V3
steps: Execute supply and withdraw on any Aave V3 market
Query current supply APY, utilization rate, and available liquidity for all markets
Transactions include correct approval flow (ERC-20 approve then supply)
Works on Sepolia testnet with Aave V3 testnet deployment
Gas estimation accurate within 10% of actual cost
All interactions go through the Smart Wallet

description: Event reporter — publishes the result of every transaction execution to execution:results in a standardized format for Python monitoring consumption
steps: Every submitted transaction gets a result published (success or failure)
Result includes: TX hash, status (confirmed/failed/reverted), fill price, actual gas used, block number, timestamp
Failed transactions include decoded revert reason when possible
Results published within 1 block of transaction confirmation
Results conform to shared JSON schema for execution:results

description: Real-time price feed — multi-source token price ingestion, normalization, and caching with oracle manipulation guards (>2% deviation rejection, TWAP fallback)
steps: Prices for ETH, WBTC, major stablecoins, and Aave-supported tokens updated at least every 30 seconds
At least 2 independent price sources per token
Prices cached in Redis with TTL; stale prices flagged, not silently used
Prices with >2% deviation between sources are rejected (oracle manipulation guard)
TWAP calculation available over configurable windows (5m, 1h, 24h)
All timestamps are UTC

description: Gas price monitor — track and predict Ethereum gas prices for transaction timing optimization and profitability calculations
steps: Current gas price (fast, standard, slow) cached in Redis and updated every block
24h rolling average maintained for circuit breaker comparison
Gas cost estimation available as a function call for any proposed transaction
Historical gas data stored for pattern analysis (time-of-day, day-of-week)
Alert triggered when gas price exceeds configurable threshold

description: DeFi protocol metrics collector — collect and normalize protocol-specific metrics (Aave rates, Uniswap pool state, Lido yields, protocol TVLs) for strategy decision-making
steps: Aave: utilization rate, supply APY, borrow APY, available liquidity per market — updated every 5 minutes minimum
Uniswap V3: pool reserves, current tick, fee tier, 24h volume for target pairs
Lido: current stETH APY and queue status
Protocol TVL from DeFi Llama API or on-chain — updated hourly minimum
All metrics normalized into unified internal format regardless of source
Data source failures handled gracefully: use cached value plus alert, don't block operations

description: On-chain position reconciliation — query actual on-chain positions via Alchemy and compare against agent-state.json to detect and resolve discrepancies
steps: Queries all known wallet positions on-chain (token balances, Aave deposits, LP positions)
Compares on-chain state against agent-state.json records
Detects discrepancies: missing positions, unexpected balances, confirmed-but-unrecorded TXs
Reconciliation runs on every startup and on configurable schedule (e.g. every 30 minutes)
Discrepancies logged with full context and trigger alerts
Auto-reconciliation for simple cases (TX confirmed while offline); manual review for complex ones

description: Aave lending optimization — Tier 1 strategy that automatically rotates supplied assets across Aave V3 markets to capture highest risk-adjusted supply APY
steps: Identifies highest-yield Aave supply markets for whitelisted assets
Calculates net yield after gas cost of rotation
Only rotates when net improvement exceeds minimum threshold (e.g. >0.5% APY improvement)
Respects exposure limits (max 40% in any single protocol)
Generates structured orders for TS executor with exact supply/withdraw parameters
Tracks historical performance of each market supplied to
Works on Sepolia with Aave V3 testnet deployment

description: Strategy lifecycle manager — manages activation, evaluation, pausing, and retirement of all strategies with structured status tracking and one-adjustment-per-cycle enforcement
steps: Each strategy has a status field persisted in agent-state.json
Valid transitions enforced: evaluating→active, active→paused, paused→active, active→retired
Only one strategy adjustment (rebalance, entry, exit) per decision cycle
Strategy performance tracked independently (P&L, Sharpe, drawdown per strategy)
Underperforming strategies flagged for review; auto-pause if losses exceed per-strategy threshold
New strategy tier activation requires human approval (Human-in-the-Loop gate)

description: Portfolio allocator — manages capital allocation across strategy tiers: Tier 1 (50-60%), Tier 2 (25-35%), Tier 3 (10-20%), with exposure limits and minimum stablecoin reserves
steps: Allocates capital according to tier percentages with configurable bounds
Maintains minimum 15% in stablecoins/liquid reserves at all times
Enforces max 40% in any single protocol
Enforces max 60% in any single asset (excluding stablecoins)
Calculates available capital for new positions accounting for all active positions and reserves
Rebalancing triggers when allocation drifts beyond configurable threshold (e.g. ±5%)

description: Position tracker — real-time view of all open positions across all chains and protocols with entry price, current value, unrealized P&L, and protocol-specific metrics
steps: Tracks every open position with: entry price, entry time, current value, unrealized P&L
Protocol-specific tracking: Aave (supply amount, earned interest), Uniswap (tick range, fees earned, IL), Lido (staked amount, rewards)
Updated in real-time as execution:results arrive from Redis
Queryable by strategy, protocol, chain, and asset
Position history maintained in PostgreSQL for audit trail

description: Agent state persistence — agent-state.json as persistent knowledge store, updated after every action with portfolio state, strategy statuses, and operational metadata
steps: State file written after every action (trade, rebalance, strategy status change)
State includes: portfolio positions, strategy statuses, last reconciliation timestamp, operational flags
Atomic writes (write to temp file then rename) to prevent corruption on crash
State file is human-readable JSON for debugging
State backed up to PostgreSQL periodically, not just local filesystem
State is versioned with schema version field for forward compatibility

description: Startup recovery sequence — deterministic recovery on every start: load state → check Redis for unprocessed messages → reconcile on-chain state → health check → resume or diagnostic mode
steps: Reads agent-state.json and restores portfolio knowledge
Checks Redis Streams for unprocessed execution:orders and execution:results
Queries on-chain state via Alchemy to verify positions match internal records
Reconciles discrepancies (e.g. TX confirmed while offline)
Runs health checks on all connected protocols
Resumes normal operation if all checks pass; enters diagnostic mode with alert if not
Recovery completes in under 60 seconds (target), under 5 minutes (hard limit)

description: Diagnostic mode — safe mode entered on irreconcilable state or critical failures: no new trades, positions held, full state dump logged, owner alerted
steps: Triggered by: startup reconciliation failure, critical circuit breaker, manual command
All new trading operations halted
Existing positions maintained (not unwound — that's emergency shutdown's job)
Full state dump written to logs: agent-state.json contents, Redis stream state, on-chain positions
Alert sent to owner with summary of what went wrong
Manual exit from diagnostic mode required — no auto-resume

description: Structured logging — every trade, signal, risk decision, and system event logged in structured JSON with correlation IDs linking the full lifecycle
steps: All logs are structured JSON, not free-text
Every log entry includes: timestamp (UTC), service name, event type, correlation ID
Correlation IDs link full lifecycle: market event → strategy signal → risk check → order → execution → result
Log levels used correctly: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, CRITICAL
Logs written to both stdout (for Railway) and rotating log files
Sensitive data (private keys, full wallet addresses) never logged

description: Drawdown circuit breaker — monitors portfolio value relative to peak, warns at 15% drawdown, triggers full emergency shutdown and unwind to stablecoins at 20%
steps: Tracks portfolio peak value continuously
Calculates current drawdown from peak in real-time
At 15% drawdown: alert sent, new position entries paused
At 20% drawdown: all positions unwound to stablecoins, all trading halted, alert sent
Cannot be overridden programmatically — requires manual restart after investigation
Drawdown calculation accounts for all positions across all chains

description: Per-position loss limit — closes any position that loses >10% of entry value and puts the corresponding strategy into 24h cooldown
steps: Every position has a tracked entry value
Loss calculated relative to entry value, updated with each price feed refresh
At >10% loss: position closed, strategy enters 24h cooldown
Cooldown enforced — strategy cannot open new positions until cooldown expires
Loss events logged with full context: entry price, exit price, duration, strategy

description: Gas spike circuit breaker — pauses non-urgent operations when gas exceeds 3x 24h average, allows urgent operations (stop-loss), queues others for later
steps: Compares current gas price against 24h rolling average
At >3x average: pauses non-urgent operations (new entries, rebalancing, harvesting)
Urgent operations (stop-loss exits, emergency withdrawals) exempt from pause
Queued operations held and executed when gas returns below threshold
Alert sent when gas pause activates and when it deactivates

description: Transaction failure rate monitor — pauses execution and enters diagnostic mode if more than 3 transactions fail within one hour
steps: Counts failed transactions in a rolling 1-hour window
At >3 failures/hour: pauses all execution, enters diagnostic mode, sends alert
Failure reasons logged and categorized (revert, out of gas, timeout, nonce issue)
System does not auto-resume — requires manual investigation
Distinguishes between parameter errors and systemic failures (network issues)

description: Smart contract allowlist — TS executor maintains strict list of approved contract addresses; orders targeting non-allowlisted contracts are rejected
steps: Allowlist maintained as configuration file, not hardcoded
TS executor validates target contract address against allowlist before every transaction
Non-allowlisted contracts rejected with error published to execution:results
Allowlist updates require service restart (intentional friction, not hot-reloadable)
Allowlist includes Aave V3, Uniswap V3, Lido, and their periphery contracts
Separate allowlists per chain (mainnet, Arbitrum, Base, Solana)

description: Oracle manipulation guard — multi-source price validation that rejects operations when prices deviate >2% between sources, with TWAP fallback
steps: Every price used in a trading decision validated against at least 2 independent sources
Rejects prices with >2% deviation between sources
TWAP-based smoothing available as fallback when spot prices are unreliable
Logs all price rejections with source details for post-hoc analysis
Does not block operations when one source is temporarily unavailable (graceful degradation + alert)

description: Exposure limit enforcement — enforces portfolio-level concentration limits: max 40% per protocol, max 60% per asset, min 15% stablecoin reserves
steps: Max 40% of portfolio in any single protocol checked before every new position
Max 60% of portfolio in any single asset (excluding stablecoins) enforced
Min 15% in stablecoins/liquid reserves at all times
Orders that would violate limits rejected before reaching TS executor
Current exposure levels queryable for dashboard display
Limits are configurable but changes are logged for audit trail

description: Sepolia testnet integration suite — full end-to-end validation of all P1 features on Sepolia with no real capital
steps: End-to-end flow works on Sepolia: detect opportunity → evaluate → approve → execute → confirm → log
Aave supply/withdraw cycle completes successfully on Sepolia
Agent startup recovery works after simulated crash (kill process, restart, verify state)
Circuit breakers trigger correctly when simulated thresholds are exceeded
Redis schema validation catches malformed messages

description: PostgreSQL database — persistent storage for trade history, audit trail, portfolio snapshots, and performance metrics as the system of record
steps: Database schema defined for: trades, portfolio_snapshots, strategy_performance, alerts
Every executed trade written to PostgreSQL with full context (strategy, prices, gas, slippage, result)
Portfolio snapshots taken at configurable intervals (minimum hourly)
Query performance acceptable for dashboard reads (under 200ms for common queries)
Migrations framework in place for schema evolution
Backup strategy defined (at minimum Railway's managed backups)

description: L2 chain listeners (Arbitrum, Base) — extend chain listener to L2 networks with L2-specific RPC endpoints, block times, and protocol event parsing
steps: WebSocket connections to Arbitrum and Base via Alchemy
Events from L2s published to same market:events channel with chain identifier
L2-specific protocols (GMX events, Aerodrome pool changes) parsed correctly
Listener handles L2-specific quirks (faster block times, different finality guarantees)
Can be enabled/disabled per-chain via configuration

description: Uniswap V3 protocol adapter — mint/burn concentrated liquidity positions, collect fees, query pool state (price, tick, liquidity depth)
steps: Mint a new concentrated liquidity position with specified price range
Increase/decrease liquidity in an existing position
Collect accumulated fees from position
Burn (close) a position entirely
Pool state queries return: current price, active tick, liquidity at each tick range, 24h volume
All swaps routed through Flashbots Protect

description: Lido staking adapter — stake ETH to receive stETH, wrap/unwrap stETH↔wstETH, query staking APY and queue status
steps: Stake ETH via Lido and receive stETH
Wrap stETH to wstETH and unwrap back
Query current staking APY and queue status
Handle stETH rebasing model correctly in position tracking
Works on testnet with Lido Sepolia deployment or mock

description: L2 protocol adapters (GMX, Aerodrome) — protocol adapters for L2-specific DeFi: GMX perpetuals on Arbitrum, Aerodrome LP on Base
steps: GMX adapter: open/close positions, query funding rates, manage margin
Aerodrome adapter: provide liquidity, collect fees/rewards, query pool APYs
Both adapters use same order schema and result reporting as Ethereum adapters
L2 gas estimation accounts for L1 data posting costs (Arbitrum/Base specific)

description: L2 data pipeline extension — extend data pipeline to ingest and normalize data from Arbitrum and Base with L2-specific gas models and protocol metrics
steps: Price feeds cover L2-specific tokens and L2 deployments of existing tokens
L2 gas estimation accounts for L1 data posting costs
GMX metrics collected: funding rates, open interest, liquidation levels
Aerodrome metrics collected: pool APYs, reward emissions, liquidity depth
All L2 data normalized into same internal format as mainnet data

description: Liquid staking strategy — Tier 1 strategy: stake ETH via Lido (stETH) or RocketPool (rETH), optionally deploy derivatives into composable yield (Aave supply, LP)
steps: Evaluates staking APY for Lido vs RocketPool and selects optimal
Executes staking and receives liquid staking derivative
Optionally deploys derivatives into composable yield (Aave supply, LP)
Tracks total yield: base staking reward + derivative yield
Monitors for de-peg risk (stETH/ETH ratio) and exits if deviation exceeds threshold
Accounts for exit queue times in position management

description: Uniswap V3 concentrated liquidity — Tier 2 strategy with dynamic range management based on historical volatility, auto-rebalancing, fee compounding, and IL exit threshold
steps: Identifies target pools based on volume, fee tier, and TVL
Calculates optimal price range based on historical volatility (±1-2 standard deviations)
Mints position with calculated range
Monitors price relative to range; triggers rebalance when price approaches boundary (e.g. 80% consumed)
Rebalance: withdraw, recalculate range, re-mint — accounting for gas + impermanent loss
Auto-compounds collected fees at configurable intervals
Exits position if impermanent loss exceeds threshold (e.g. >5%)

description: Yield farming with auto-compound — Tier 2 strategy: participate in yield farming with automatic harvesting and compounding of rewards to maximize effective APY
steps: Identifies active yield farming opportunities across whitelisted protocols
Calculates real APY after: gas for harvesting, token swap costs, reward token price volatility
Harvests rewards when accumulated value exceeds gas cost by configurable multiplier (e.g. 3x)
Compounds rewards: swaps reward tokens and re-deposits into farming position
Monitors for reward program expiration and exits before rewards end
Accounts for reward token volatility in APY calculations, not just current price

description: Rebalancing engine — automatically rebalances portfolio when allocation drifts beyond thresholds, one adjustment per cycle, gas-aware
steps: Detects when portfolio allocation deviates from target by more than configurable threshold
Generates rebalancing plan: which positions to reduce, which to increase, in what order
Executes one adjustment per cycle (not full rebalance in one shot)
Accounts for gas costs in rebalancing decisions (don't rebalance if gas > benefit)
Logs every rebalancing decision with full reasoning for audit

description: Human-in-the-loop approval gates — high-impact actions require explicit owner approval via Discord before execution; agent continues other operations while waiting
steps: Approval required for: new protocol deployment, trades >15% of portfolio, new strategy tier activation
Approval request sent via Discord with: action description, amounts, risk context, estimated impact
Agent continues other operations while waiting for approval (doesn't block everything)
Approval timeout configurable (default 24h); action cancelled on timeout
Emergency override commands via Discord: pause all, force-unwind, withdraw all
All approvals and rejections logged with timestamp

description: Discord alert system — push notifications for critical events, daily performance summaries, anomaly alerts, and inline buttons for human-in-the-loop approvals
steps: Circuit breaker triggers sent with: trigger type, threshold, action taken
Large position changes reported with: before/after values, strategy, reasoning
Daily performance summary: portfolio value, daily P&L, active strategies, top/bottom performers
Anomaly alerts: unexpected on-chain activity, failed reconciliation, service restarts
Configurable alert severity levels (not everything sends a push notification)
Inline keyboard buttons for human-in-the-loop approvals (approve/reject)

description: Performance dashboard — real-time tracking of portfolio value, cumulative P&L, Sharpe ratio, strategy-level attribution, and gas costs
steps: Tracks and displays: portfolio value (USD), cumulative P&L, annualized return (APY)
Sharpe ratio calculated over rolling windows (7d, 30d, all-time)
Strategy-level attribution: which strategies contributed how much to total return
Gas cost tracking: total gas spent, gas as percentage of returns
Max drawdown tracking: current drawdown from peak, historical worst drawdown
Data stored in PostgreSQL; queryable via API for frontend consumption

description: Anomaly detection — automated detection of unusual gas consumption, unexpected balance changes, protocol behavior changes, or performance degradation
steps: Detects: unexpected wallet balance changes, gas spend anomalies, strategy performance degradation
Monitors protocol health signals: TVL drops, utilization spikes, governance proposals
Flags when actual execution deviates significantly from expected (price impact, gas, timing)
Anomalies trigger alerts with severity classification
False positive rate is manageable (not alert fatigue)

description: Protocol TVL monitor — monitors TVL of all protocols with active positions; withdraws capital if TVL drops >30% in 24h
steps: TVL tracked for every protocol with active positions
24h rolling change calculated continuously
At >30% drop: withdraw all capital from affected protocol, alert sent
Withdrawal prioritized over other operations (treated as urgent)
Tracks TVL from DeFi Llama plus on-chain verification (not single-source dependent)

description: Claude API market sentiment analysis — minimal, cost-conscious sentiment signal on configurable schedule (every 4-6h), used to augment quantitative signals, never sole trade trigger
steps: Calls Claude API on configurable schedule (e.g. every 4-6 hours), not per-minute
Input: recent market events, protocol news, on-chain anomalies
Output: structured sentiment signal (bullish/bearish/neutral with confidence score)
Sentiment signal is one input among many — never the sole trigger for a trade
API costs tracked and logged; alert if costs exceed configurable monthly cap
Graceful degradation: if API unavailable, agent operates normally without sentiment signal

description: Railway production deployment — deploy dual-service architecture to Railway with service isolation, managed Redis, secrets management, auto-deploy, and health checks
steps: railway.toml configured for both services
Redis provisioned as Railway plugin or managed instance with persistence enabled
Environment variables managed through Railway secrets, not committed to repo
Both services auto-deploy on git push to main
Health check endpoints on both services; Railway restarts unhealthy containers
Logs accessible through Railway dashboard
Cost stays within budget (infrastructure portion of $250-$1,000 CAD)

description: Solana chain listener — extend listener to Solana using @solana/web3.js with account-based event model (program logs, account changes) for Marinade, Raydium, Jupiter
steps: Connection to Solana RPC (Alchemy or Helius) with WebSocket subscriptions
Monitors relevant Solana programs: Marinade, Raydium, Jupiter
Account change notifications parsed into same normalized event format
Events published to market:events with chain: solana identifier
Handles Solana-specific reliability issues (RPC instability, slot skipping)

description: Flash loan executor — execute atomic flash loan arbitrage: borrow from Aave, swap across DEXs, repay loan + fee, capture profit in a single transaction that reverts if unprofitable
steps: Execute Aave V3 flash loan with custom callback contract
Callback contract executes multi-step swap logic (buy on DEX A, sell on DEX B)
Transaction is atomic — reverts entirely if profit threshold not met on-chain
Gas cost of entire flash loan TX estimated before submission
Profit calculation accounts for: flash loan fee, gas cost, slippage
Routed through Flashbots Protect

description: Solana transaction executor — extend executor to Solana via @solana/web3.js for Marinade staking, Raydium LP, and Jupiter swap aggregation
steps: Submit transactions to Solana via Alchemy or Helius RPC
Supports Marinade: stake SOL, unstake, query rewards
Supports Raydium: add/remove liquidity, collect fees
Supports Jupiter: execute aggregated swaps with slippage protection
Transaction confirmation handles Solana confirmation levels (processed → confirmed → finalized)
Results published to execution:results in same format as Ethereum transactions

description: Solana data pipeline extension — extend data pipeline for Solana's account-based model with SOL/SPL token prices, Marinade/Raydium/Jupiter metrics, and priority fee tracking
steps: SOL and SPL token prices integrated into unified price feed
Marinade metrics: staking APY, unstake queue, mSOL exchange rate
Raydium metrics: pool reserves, concentrated liquidity positions, fees
Jupiter: available swap routes and quotes for target pairs
Solana-specific gas (priority fees, compute units) tracked and cached

description: Flash loan arbitrage — Tier 3 strategy: identify and execute atomic cross-DEX arbitrage using flash loans with zero capital at risk
steps: Continuously monitors price discrepancies across DEXs for target pairs
Calculates profit after: flash loan fee, gas cost, slippage on both legs
Only executes when estimated profit exceeds minimum threshold (configurable)
Transaction is atomic — reverts if profit condition not met on-chain
Execution latency from opportunity detection to TX submission under 2 seconds
Tracks hit rate (opportunities detected vs. successfully captured) for strategy evaluation
Handles competition gracefully (many opportunities captured by faster searchers)

description: Lending rate arbitrage — Tier 3 strategy: borrow at lower rate on one protocol, supply at higher rate on another, unwind when spread compresses
steps: Monitors borrow and supply rates across Aave, Compound, and L2 lending protocols
Identifies spreads exceeding minimum profitability threshold after gas and fees
Executes carry trade: borrow on protocol A, supply on protocol B
Continuously monitors spread; unwinds if spread compresses below threshold
Manages health factor on borrow side to avoid liquidation (min health factor configurable, e.g. >1.5)
Accounts for variable rate risk (rates can change every block)

description: Tax reporting engine — calculate capital gains/losses for all trades compatible with Canadian tax requirements using Adjusted Cost Base (ACB) method
steps: Tracks cost basis for every acquired asset (including gas fees as part of cost basis)
Calculates realized gain/loss on every disposal (sell, swap, LP exit)
Supports ACB (Adjusted Cost Base) method for Canadian tax reporting
Exportable report in CSV format with: date, asset, amount, proceeds, cost basis, gain/loss
Handles DeFi-specific tax events: LP entry/exit, staking rewards, yield farming rewards, flash loan fees
Full audit trail linking every tax event to original transaction hash

description: P&L attribution report — break down total portfolio performance by strategy, protocol, chain, and time period to answer 'where did my returns come from?'
steps: Attribution by strategy: how much each strategy contributed to total return
Attribution by protocol: Aave vs Uniswap vs Lido contribution
Attribution by chain: Ethereum vs L2 vs Solana contribution
Time-based: daily, weekly, monthly P&L breakdown
Includes gas costs as line item (total gas, gas per strategy)
Exportable as CSV or JSON

description: Historical stress testing — replay March 2020 COVID crash, May 2021 crypto crash, and November 2022 FTX collapse through the system to validate circuit breakers hold
steps: Historical price and on-chain data available for each scenario for replay
System runs in simulation mode (no real transactions, but full decision pipeline executes)
Drawdown circuit breaker activates at correct thresholds during each scenario
Portfolio drawdown stays within 20% hard limit across all simulated scenarios
Results report generated: max drawdown, time to recovery, positions held during crisis

description: ML gas prediction model — machine learning model predicting short-term gas prices (1h, 4h, 24h) based on historical patterns for transaction timing optimization
steps: Model trained on minimum 6 months of historical gas data
Predicts gas price for next 1h, 4h, and 24h windows
Prediction accuracy within 20% of actual gas price 80% of the time
Used by strategy engine to time non-urgent operations for low-gas windows
Model retrained on configurable schedule (e.g. weekly)
Fallback to simple heuristics if model fails or degrades
